extraction_reasoning: The litigation table lists matters involving the Company: criminal 2, regulatory/statutory 0, tax 3, and civil 1; the separate Directors table has no criminal or regulatory matters and one personal tax matter, which is excluded from the company-group tax count. I counted only the Company rows and excluded the Directors' tax proceeding; the quantified amount against the Company is ₹50.00 million civil + ₹0.20 million criminal + ₹1.56 million tax = ₹51.76 million, or ₹5.176 crore, while all 'By' rows are absent. No stated total contingent-liabilities figure is disclosed; only a ₹77.60 million corporate guarantee and a contingent-liabilities-to-net-worth percentage of 1.78% are provided, as of December 31, 2023. Concentration percentages disclosed in the section relate to a specific e-commerce distribution agreement, not top-five or top-ten company-wide customers, so those fields are null. The litigation source unit is ₹ million; conversion to crore is by dividing by 10 (₹51.76 million ÷ 10 = ₹5.176 crore).
